title: Phase 6: New Commands
overview: Add YouTube transcript extraction, download inline base64 support, and Netscape cookie file import to browse_cli. Phase 6 of the camoufox integration roadmap. All three features are runtime-agnostic — they work with any browser runtime (Playwright, Camoufox, Rebrowser). YouTube transcripts use yt-dlp as fast path (no browser needed), falling back to browser-based caption intercept. Download inline returns file content as base64 in the CLI response. Netscape cookie import adds support for .txt cookie files exported by browser extensions (EditThisCookie, Get cookies.txt, etc.).
scopeChallenge: Three independent features sharing the command pipeline. YouTube transcript is the largest: new handler file (src/commands/meta/youtube.ts), new helper module (src/browser/youtube.ts), and registry entry. The yt-dlp fast path spawns a child process with a sanitized environment — security-critical. Download inline is a small extension to the existing download handler in src/commands/write.ts — add --inline flag that reads the saved file and returns base64 instead of the path. Netscape cookie import extends the existing cookie-import command in src/commands/meta/auth.ts — the current implementation only handles Chromium SQLite databases. Need to detect Netscape format (.txt files with tab-separated fields) and parse them into Playwright-compatible cookies. The Netscape parser is a new function in src/browser/cookie-import.ts alongside the existing Chromium decryption pipeline.

prerequisites.assumption: CommandRegistry in src/automation/registry.ts supports meta command registration via m() helper
prerequisites.verification: registry.ts:24 — m() helper creates CommandSpec with category 'meta'

prerequisites.assumption: Meta command dispatcher (src/commands/meta/index.ts) routes to handler modules via command set membership
prerequisites.verification: meta/index.ts:23-43 — routing sets for TABS, SCREENSHOTS, RECORDING, SESSIONS, etc.

prerequisites.assumption: Download command handler exists in src/commands/write.ts with save-to-disk logic
prerequisites.verification: write.ts:732-744 — case 'download' saves file via download.saveAs()

prerequisites.assumption: Cookie import exists for Chromium SQLite databases only (Chrome, Arc, Brave, Edge on macOS)
prerequisites.verification: cookie-import.ts:86-91 — BROWSER_REGISTRY only lists Chromium browsers; no Netscape .txt parsing

prerequisites.assumption: yt-dlp binary available on developer machines for YouTube fast path
prerequisites.verification: yt-dlp is optional — fallback to browser-based intercept when not available

prerequisites.assumption: Test infrastructure has download fixture (test/fixtures/download-link.html) and test server with /download/test.txt route

nonGoals: YouTube playlist or channel batch transcript extraction
Download manager with queue/retry/resume (only single-file inline base64)
Cookie encryption/vault for Netscape-imported cookies (they are plain text)
Firefox cookie database decryption (only Netscape .txt format added)
Browser-based YouTube transcript fallback (Phase 6 only implements yt-dlp path; browser intercept is a future enhancement)

contracts.producer: src/browser/youtube.ts exports: detectYtDlp, ensureYtDlp, ytDlpTranscript, parseJson3, parseVtt, parseXml
contracts.consumer: src/commands/meta/youtube.ts imports helpers, calls ensureYtDlp + ytDlpTranscript
contracts.shape: ytDlpTranscript(url, lang?) returns { status: 'ok', transcript, video_title, language, total_words } | { status: 'error', code, message }
contracts.consistencyRule: Handler catches yt-dlp not available error and returns user-friendly message with install instructions
Inline flag reads file after saveAs, then deletes temp file. Max size 20MB (MAX_DOWNLOAD_INLINE_BYTES). Exceeding returns error with file path fallback.

contracts.shape: parseNetscapeCookieFile(text) returns PlaywrightCookie[]; importNetscapeCookies(filePath, domain?) returns ImportResult
contracts.consistencyRule: Netscape format detected by --file flag. Domain filtering optional. Cookies converted to Playwright format and added to context.

existingCodeLeverage.subProblem: yt-dlp process spawning with safe environment
Cookie import handler and auth.ts dispatcher

failureModes.risk: yt-dlp not installed on user machine — transcript command fails with unhelpful error
failureModes.mitigation: ensureYtDlp() returns false when binary not found. Handler returns clear message: 'yt-dlp not installed. Install: brew install yt-dlp (macOS) or pip install yt-dlp'. No browser fallback in Phase 6.

failureModes.risk: yt-dlp subprocess hangs or takes too long on slow networks
failureModes.mitigation: All yt-dlp calls have explicit timeouts (15s for title, 30s for subtitles). Safe environment (only PATH, HOME, LANG). maxBuffer 4MB to prevent memory issues.

failureModes.risk: Download inline exceeds memory for large files (>20MB)
failureModes.mitigation: MAX_DOWNLOAD_INLINE_BYTES = 20MB hard limit. Files exceeding this return error with the disk path so caller can read the file directly.

failureModes.risk: Netscape cookie file has malformed lines or unexpected encoding
failureModes.mitigation: Parser skips malformed lines (less than 7 tab-separated fields). BOM stripping for UTF-8 with BOM. Invalid lines logged as warnings, not errors.

failureModes.risk: YouTube video has no captions — yt-dlp returns empty
failureModes.mitigation: Handler returns structured error: 'No captions available for this video' with video URL and title. Not a crash — clean error response.

testCoverageMap.codepath: parseJson3() parses YouTube JSON3 caption format into timestamped lines
parseVtt() parses WebVTT caption format into timestamped lines
parseXml() parses XML/srv3 caption format into timestamped lines
normalizeYoutubeUrl() validates and normalizes YouTube URLs
download --inline with oversized file returns error with path fallback
parseNetscapeCookieFile() parses Netscape .txt cookies into PlaywrightCookie[]
parseNetscapeCookieFile() skips comment lines and malformed entries
cookie-import --file <path> loads Netscape cookies into browser context
youtube-transcript command registered in CommandRegistry as meta command

tasks.title: YouTube yt-dlp detection and caption parsers
tasks.description:
Create `src/browser/youtube.ts` with yt-dlp binary detection, safe child process spawning, and three caption format parsers (JSON3, VTT, XML/srv3). Port from camofox-browser/lib/youtube.js with TypeScript types.

Key functions to implement:
1. `detectYtDlp()` — scan candidate paths (/usr/local/bin/yt-dlp, etc.), cache result
2. `ensureYtDlp()` — lazy re-detect on each call if initial detection failed
3. `hasYtDlp()` — synchronous check of cached detection result
4. `buildSafeEnv()` — whitelist only PATH, HOME, LANG, LC_ALL, LC_CTYPE, TMPDIR
5. `runYtDlp(binary, args, timeoutMs)` — spawn with safe env, maxBuffer 4MB, timeout
6. `normalizeYoutubeUrl(rawUrl)` — validate URL scheme (https/http), host (youtube.com, youtu.be)
7. `normalizeLanguage(rawLang)` — validate language code regex, default 'en'
8. `ytDlpTranscript(url, lang?, proxyUrl?)` — full transcript extraction pipeline:
   - Get video title via --print %(title)s
   - Download subtitles to temp dir (--write-sub --write-auto-sub --sub-lang --sub-format json3)
   - Find subtitle file (.json3, .vtt, .srv3)
   - Parse with appropriate parser
   - Return { status, transcript, video_title, language, total_words } or error
   - Clean up temp dir in finally block
9. `parseJson3(content)` — parse YouTube JSON3 format (events[].segs[].utf8 with tStartMs timestamps)
10. `parseVtt(content)` — parse WebVTT format (strip headers, extract timestamps and text, decode HTML entities)
11. `parseXml(content)` — parse XML/srv3 format (<text start="..."> elements, decode HTML entities)

Export barrel in `src/browser/index.ts`.

Security: All yt-dlp args are constructed from validated values (no user input interpolation). Environment is whitelisted. temp dirs are cleaned up in finally blocks.

Reference: camofox-browser/lib/youtube.js (lines 1-301)
acceptanceCriteria: parseJson3() converts YouTube JSON3 caption data into '[MM:SS] text' format
parseVtt() converts WebVTT caption data into '[MM:SS] text' format, stripping HTML tags and decoding entities
parseXml() converts XML/srv3 caption data into '[MM:SS] text' format
normalizeYoutubeUrl() rejects non-YouTube URLs and non-http(s) schemes
buildSafeEnv() only includes whitelisted environment variables
ytDlpTranscript() cleans up temp directory even on error (finally block)
All functions have TypeScript types — no any types
tasks.rollbackPlan: Delete src/browser/youtube.ts, revert src/browser/index.ts barrel export

tasks.title: YouTube transcript meta command and registry entry
tasks.description:
Create `src/commands/meta/youtube.ts` as the command handler, register `youtube-transcript` in the command registry, and wire it into the meta dispatcher.

**Handler (src/commands/meta/youtube.ts):**
1. Export `handleYoutubeCommand(command, args, bm)` following the pattern of other meta handlers (sim.ts, auth.ts)
2. Parse args: first positional arg is the YouTube URL, optional --lang <code> flag
3. Call ensureYtDlp() — if false, return clear error with install instructions for macOS (brew install yt-dlp) and generic (pip install yt-dlp)
4. Call ytDlpTranscript(url, lang) from src/browser/youtube.ts
5. On success: format output as 'Title: <title>\nLanguage: <lang>\nWords: <count>\n\n<transcript>'
6. On error (no captions): return the error message from ytDlpTranscript result

**Registry (src/automation/registry.ts):**
Add `m('youtube-transcript', ...)` entry near the other meta commands. Include:
- description: 'Extract YouTube video transcript'
- usage: '<url> [--lang <code>]'
- safeToRetry: true
- mcp schema with url (required) and lang (optional) properties
- argDecode function

**Meta dispatcher (src/commands/meta/index.ts):**
1. Import handleYoutubeCommand from './youtube'
2. Add YOUTUBE_COMMANDS = new Set(['youtube-transcript'])
3. Add routing block before the catch-all throw

The command is a meta command because it primarily extracts data (like inspection commands) and uses a tool external to the browser (yt-dlp). It is safe to retry since it has no side effects.
acceptanceCriteria: youtube-transcript appears in META_COMMANDS set (derived from registry)
browse youtube-transcript <youtube-url> returns transcript text when yt-dlp is available
browse youtube-transcript without URL throws usage error
MCP schema has url (required) and lang (optional) properties
When yt-dlp is not installed, returns helpful error message with install instructions
tasks.rollbackPlan: Delete src/commands/meta/youtube.ts, revert registry.ts and index.ts changes

tasks.title: Download inline base64 extension
tasks.description:
Extend the existing download command in `src/commands/write.ts` (line 732-744) to support a `--inline` flag that returns the downloaded file content as a base64 data URI instead of just saving to disk.

**Changes to write.ts download handler:**
1. Parse --inline flag from args (before the selector/savePath extraction)
2. When --inline is set:
   a. Save file to temp path (os.tmpdir() + random UUID) instead of user path
   b. Read the file with fs.readFile()
   c. Check file size against MAX_DOWNLOAD_INLINE_BYTES (20MB)
   d. If under limit: encode as base64, guess MIME type from filename, return `data:<mime>;base64,<encoded>`
   e. If over limit: return error `File too large for inline (${size} bytes). Saved to: ${tempPath}`
   f. Clean up temp file after reading (unless over limit — leave it for the caller)
3. Without --inline: existing behavior unchanged

**Changes to registry.ts download entry (line 412-420):**
1. Add `inline` boolean property to inputSchema
2. Update argDecode to handle inline flag: if (p.inline) args.push('--inline')
3. Update MCP description to mention --inline option

MIME type guessing: reuse simple extension-based mapping (.png -> image/png, .jpg -> image/jpeg, etc., default application/octet-stream). Implement as a local helper in write.ts or extract to a shared utility.

Reference: camofox-browser/lib/downloads.js:118-150 (getDownloadsList with includeData flag)
acceptanceCriteria: browse download <selector> --inline returns data:<mime>;base64,<encoded> string
browse download <selector> <path> (without --inline) behavior unchanged — saves to path
Files over 20MB with --inline return error with saved temp path
MCP schema includes inline boolean property
Temp file is cleaned up after successful inline read
tasks.rollbackPlan: Revert write.ts download handler and registry.ts download entry

tasks.title: Netscape cookie format parity
tasks.description:
Add Netscape cookie file (.txt) import support to the existing cookie-import command. The current implementation only handles Chromium browser SQLite databases. This adds a --file flag to import from Netscape-format text files (the de facto standard exported by browser extensions like EditThisCookie, Get cookies.txt, Cookie-Editor, etc.).

**New functions in src/browser/cookie-import.ts:**
1. `parseNetscapeCookieFile(text: string): PlaywrightCookie[]` — Parse Netscape format:
   - Strip BOM (\uFEFF)
   - Skip empty lines and comment lines (starting with #, except #HttpOnly_)
   - Handle #HttpOnly_ prefix (strip prefix, set httpOnly flag)
   - Split each line by tabs — expect 7+ fields: domain, subdomains-flag, path, secure, expires, name, value
   - Convert 'TRUE'/'FALSE' strings to booleans for secure flag
   - Convert expires to Unix timestamp (number)
   - Map sameSite to 'Lax' default (Netscape format doesn't specify sameSite)
   - Return array of PlaywrightCookie objects

2. `importNetscapeCookies(filePath: string, domain?: string): Promise<ImportResult>` — Read and parse:
   - Validate file exists and size < 5MB
   - Read file as UTF-8
   - Call parseNetscapeCookieFile()
   - If domain filter provided, filter cookies where domain ends with the filter
   - Return ImportResult with cookies, count, failed (0 for Netscape — no decryption), domainCounts

Export both functions from src/browser/cookie-import.ts.

**Changes to src/commands/meta/auth.ts cookie-import handler (line 73+):**
1. Detect --file <path> flag in args
2. If --file present: call importNetscapeCookies(filePath, domain?)
3. Add cookies to browser context
4. Return import count message

**Changes to src/automation/registry.ts cookie-import entry (line 660+):**
1. Add `file` string property to MCP inputSchema
2. Update argDecode to handle file flag
3. Update description to mention --file option

Reference: camofox-browser/lib/cookies.js (parseNetscapeCookieFile, readCookieFile)
acceptanceCriteria: parseNetscapeCookieFile() correctly parses standard Netscape cookie file format
parseNetscapeCookieFile() handles #HttpOnly_ prefix, BOM, and malformed lines gracefully
browse cookie-import --file cookies.txt imports cookies into browser context
browse cookie-import --file cookies.txt --domain .example.com filters by domain
Files over 5MB are rejected with clear error
MCP schema includes file property
Existing Chromium cookie import (browse cookie-import chrome) still works unchanged
tasks.rollbackPlan: Revert cookie-import.ts, auth.ts, and registry.ts changes

tasks.title: Tests for YouTube transcript, download base64, and cookie import
tasks.description:
Add tests for all three new features across test files.

**YouTube parser unit tests (test/features.test.ts):**
1. New describe block 'YouTube transcript'
2. Test parseJson3(): valid JSON3 content -> timestamped lines
3. Test parseJson3(): empty events array -> empty string
4. Test parseJson3(): malformed JSON -> null
5. Test parseVtt(): valid VTT content -> timestamped lines, HTML entities decoded
6. Test parseVtt(): VTT with nested HTML tags stripped
7. Test parseXml(): valid XML/srv3 content -> timestamped lines
8. Test normalizeYoutubeUrl(): valid YouTube URLs accepted
9. Test normalizeYoutubeUrl(): non-YouTube hosts rejected
10. Test normalizeYoutubeUrl(): non-https schemes rejected
11. Test normalizeLanguage(): valid codes pass through, invalid default to 'en'

**Download inline integration test (test/interactions.test.ts):**
1. Extend existing 'download' describe block
2. Test: download --inline returns base64 data URI (use existing /download/test.txt fixture)
3. Verify data URI format: starts with 'data:' and contains base64-encoded content
4. Decode base64 and verify content matches 'test file content'
5. Test: download without --inline still saves to disk (existing test unchanged)

**Netscape cookie parser unit tests (test/features.test.ts):**
1. New describe block 'Netscape cookie import'
2. Test parseNetscapeCookieFile(): standard 7-field tab-separated lines
3. Test parseNetscapeCookieFile(): #HttpOnly_ prefix sets httpOnly flag
4. Test parseNetscapeCookieFile(): comment lines and empty lines skipped
5. Test parseNetscapeCookieFile(): lines with fewer than 7 fields skipped
6. Test parseNetscapeCookieFile(): BOM stripped from start of file
7. Test parseNetscapeCookieFile(): secure flag TRUE/FALSE conversion

**Cookie import integration test (test/interactions.test.ts or test/features.test.ts):**
1. Create a temp Netscape cookie file with known content
2. Test cookie-import --file <path> imports cookies into context
3. Verify cookies are queryable via bm.getContext().cookies()
acceptanceCriteria: All YouTube parser unit tests pass (parseJson3, parseVtt, parseXml, normalizeYoutubeUrl, normalizeLanguage)
Download --inline integration test verifies base64 data URI format and content correctness
Netscape cookie parser unit tests cover standard format, HttpOnly prefix, BOM, malformed lines
Cookie import --file integration test verifies cookies are loaded into browser context
All existing tests continue to pass (no regressions)
